7: /// A C-compatible buffer for passing data across FFI boundaries.
8: /// The `ptr` points to the data, `len` is the valid length, and `cap` is the capacity (if managed by Rust Vec).
9: #[repr(C)]
10: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
11: pub struct OxBuffer {
12:     pub ptr: *mut u8,
13:     pub len: usize,
14:     pub cap: usize,
15: }
16: 
17: impl OxBuffer {
18:     /// Creates a generic empty buffer.
19:     pub fn empty() -> Self {
20:         Self { ptr: std::ptr::null_mut(), len: 0, cap: 0 }
21:     }
22: 
23:     /// Creates an OxBuffer from a Rust String.
24:     /// The buffer now owns the memory. To free it, call `free_ox_buffer`.
25:     pub fn from_str(s: String) -> Self {
26:         let mut v = s.into_bytes();
27:         let buf = Self {
28:             ptr: v.as_mut_ptr(),
29:             len: v.len(),
30:             cap: v.capacity(),
31:         };
32:         std::mem::forget(v); // Prevent Rust from deallocating the Vec
33:         buf
34:     }
35:     
36:     /// Converts the buffer back to a Rust String (unsafe).
37:     pub unsafe fn to_string(&self) -> String {
38:         if self.ptr.is_null() {
39:             return String::new();
40:         }
41:         let slice = std::slice::from_raw_parts(self.ptr, self.len);
42:         String::from_utf8_lossy(slice).into_owned()
43:     }
44: }
45: 
46: /// Frees the memory associated with an OxBuffer created by Rust.
47: #[no_mangle]
48: pub unsafe extern "C" fn free_ox_buffer(buf: OxBuffer) {
49:     if !buf.ptr.is_null() {
50:         let _ = Vec::from_raw_parts(buf.ptr, buf.len, buf.cap);
51:     }
52: }

54: /// Represents the hydration and persistence state of a GenericDataObject.
55: #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
56: pub enum DataObjectState {
57:     /// The object was newly created in memory and does not exist in the datastore.
58:     New,
59:     /// The object is a shell, containing only an ID. It represents a full object in the datastore.
60:     NotHydrated,
61:     /// The object is fully loaded from the datastore.
62:     Hydrated,
63:     /// The object has been modified in memory and is out of sync with the datastore.
64:     Modified,
65:     /// The object in memory is in sync with the datastore.
66:     Consistent,
67:     /// The object is marked for deletion from the datastore.
68:     Deleted,
69: }
70:

128: pub struct PersistenceDriverRegistry {
129:     drivers: HashMap<String, (Arc<dyn PersistenceDriver + Send + Sync>, DriverMetadata)>,
130: }
131: 
132: impl PersistenceDriverRegistry {
133:     fn new() -> Self {
134:         Self { drivers: HashMap::new() }
135:     }
136: 
137:     fn register_driver(&mut self, driver: Arc<dyn PersistenceDriver + Send + Sync>, metadata: DriverMetadata) {
138:         self.drivers.insert(metadata.name.clone(), (driver, metadata));
139:     }
140: 
141:     pub fn get_driver(&self, name: &str) -> Option<(Arc<dyn PersistenceDriver + Send + Sync>, DriverMetadata)> {
142:         self.drivers.get(name).cloned()
143:     }
144: 
145:     fn get_all_drivers(&self) -> Vec<DriverMetadata> {
146:         self.drivers.values().map(|(_, meta)| meta.clone()).collect()
147:     }
148: }
149: 
150: lazy_static::lazy_static! {
151:     pub static ref PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_REGISTRY: Mutex<PersistenceDriverRegistry> = Mutex::new(PersistenceDriverRegistry::new());
152: }
153: 
154: pub fn register_persistence_driver(driver: Arc<dyn PersistenceDriver + Send + Sync>, metadata: DriverMetadata) {
155:     PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_REGISTRY.lock().unwrap().register_driver(driver, metadata);
156: }
157: 
158: pub fn get_registered_drivers() -> Vec<DriverMetadata> {
159:     PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_REGISTRY.lock().unwrap().get_all_drivers()
160: }
161: 
162: pub fn unregister_persistence_driver(driver_name: &str) {
163:     PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_REGISTRY.lock().unwrap().drivers.remove(driver_name);
164: }

167: /// A trait for objects that can be persisted.
168: pub trait Persistent {
169:     fn persist(&mut self, driver_name: &str, location: &str) -> Result<(), String>;
170:     fn fetch(&self, driver_name: &str, location: &str) -> Result<Vec<GenericDataObject>, String>;
171: 
172:     /// Hydrates the object by loading its full data from the datastore.
173:     fn hydrate_object(&mut self, driver_name: &str, location: &str) -> Result<(), String>;
174: }
175: 
176: /// A trait for drivers that can persist and restore a GenericDataObject
177: pub trait PersistenceDriver {
178:     fn persist(
179:         &self,
180:         serializable_map: &HashMap<String, (String, ValueType, HashMap<String, String>)>, 
181:         location: &str,
182:     ) -> Result<(), String>;
183: 
184:     /// Restores a single object by its unique ID.
185:     fn restore(
186:         &self,
187:         location: &str,
188:         id: &str,
189:     ) -> Result<HashMap<String, (String, ValueType, HashMap<String, String>)>, String>;
190: 
191:     /// Fetches the unique IDs of objects matching a filter.
192:     fn fetch(&self, filter: &HashMap<String, (String, ValueType, HashMap<String, String>)>, location: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>, String>;
193: 
194:     fn notify_lock_status_change(&self, lock_status: &str, gdo_id: &str);
195: 
196:     fn prepare_datastore(&self, connection_info: &HashMap<String, String>) -> Result<(), String>;
197: 
198:     fn list_datasets(&self, connection_info: &HashMap<String, String>) -> Result<Vec<String>, String>;
199:     fn describe_dataset(&self, connection_info: &HashMap<String, String>, dataset_name: &str) -> Result<DataSet, String>;
200: 
201:     /// Gets the definition of connection parameters required by the driver.
202:     fn get_connection_parameters(&self) -> Vec<ConnectionParameter>;
203: 
204:     /// Executes a generic action defined by the driver (e.g., "discover_local", "validate_connection").
205:     fn call_action(&self, action: &str, _params: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {
206:         Err(format!("Action '{}' not supported by this driver", action))
207:     }
208: }
209: 
210: impl Persistent for GenericDataObject {
211:     fn persist(&mut self, driver_name: &str, location: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
212:         let registry = PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_REGISTRY.lock().unwrap();
213:         if let Some((driver, _)) = registry.get_driver(driver_name) {
214:             let map = self.to_serializable_map();
215:             driver.persist(&map, location)?;
216:             // Implicitly consistent after persist
217:             Ok(())
218:         } else {
219:             Err(format!("Driver '{}' not found", driver_name))
220:         }
221:     }
222: 
223:     fn fetch(&self, driver_name: &str, location: &str) -> Result<Vec<GenericDataObject>, String> {
224:          let registry = PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_REGISTRY.lock().unwrap();
225:         if let Some((driver, _)) = registry.get_driver(driver_name) {
226:              let filter = self.to_serializable_map();
227:              let ids = driver.fetch(&filter, location)?;
228:              
229:              let mut results = Vec::new();
230:              for id in ids {
231:                  // We can call restore on driver directly.
232:                  let restored_map = driver.restore(location, &id)?;
233:                  let obj = GenericDataObject::from_serializable_map(restored_map, &self.identifier_name);
234:                  results.push(obj);
235:              }
236:              Ok(results)
237:         } else {
238:             Err(format!("Driver '{}' not found", driver_name))
239:         }
240:     }
241: 
242:     /// Hydrates the object by loading its full data from the datastore.
243:     fn hydrate_object(&mut self, driver_name: &str, location: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
244:         let registry = PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_REGISTRY.lock().unwrap();
245:         if let Some((driver, _)) = registry.get_driver(driver_name) {
246:             let id = self.get::<String>(&self.identifier_name)
247:                 .ok_or_else(|| format!("Object missing identifier '{}'", self.identifier_name))?;
248:                 
249:             let restored_map = driver.restore(location, &id)?;
250:             // Update self from map
251:             // GenericDataObject doesn't have a "update from map" method that preserves identity?
252:             // Actually from_serializable_map creates new.
253:             // We can replace our attributes.
254:             let new_obj = GenericDataObject::from_serializable_map(restored_map, &self.identifier_name);
255:             // Assuming we can access fields or use a helper
256:             // We can iterate the map and set.
257:             // But we already have from_serializable_map logic. 
258:             // Let's rely on set_attributes if possible, or just hack it with a temporary obj.
259:             // We can't move out of new_obj easily if fields are private.
260:             // Wait, GenericDataObject fields are: attributes (private), identifier_name (public).
261:             // But to_serializable_map is public.
262:             // We need a way to bulk update.
263:             // GenericDataObject has `from_serializable_map` static method.
264:             // And `attributes` is private.
265:             // But `set` is public.
266:             // I'll assume we iterate the restored map and set fields.
267:             // Actually `PersistenceDriver::restore` returns the map.
268:             // I will iterate the map and call set_with_type.
269:             
270:              for (key, (value_str, value_type, params)) in new_obj.to_serializable_map() {
271:                  self.set_with_type(&key, value_str, value_type, Some(params));
272:              }
273:              Ok(())
274: 
275:         } else {
276:             Err(format!("Driver '{}' not found", driver_name))
277:         }
278:     }
279: }
